lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to notice that it's quieter than last year. [pause] Notice that. Don't be fooled by quiet.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Four counts. Hold.
Release through your mouth. [pause] Let the air leave you.
Your body knows what your mind won't admit. Your shoulders are up. Your jaw is clenched.
Relax the jaw. [pause] The jaw holds everything we don't say.
Breathe in. [pause] This time, I want you to count your heartbeat instead of the numbers.
How fast is it? [pause] Don't judge. Just notice.
I'm going to ask you something now, and you don't have to answer me. You answer yourself.
What are you most afraid will happen if you fall asleep?
[pause] [pause] You can keep that answer. You don't have to say it aloud.
The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] Breathe in.
The ones inside — the ones you chose to lock the door on — they are not your failure.
Repeat that. [pause] Say it. I'll wait.
I'm not a failure for staying home.
Again. [pause] Mean it this time.
I notice your breathing has changed. [pause] You're holding it now instead of flowing.
That's the body telling you something true is happening. Listen to it.
There's a reason the night is quiet. [pause] The ones who would have come early are already inside.
The ones who are coming next are preparing. [pause] Or they're sleeping. [pause] Or they've changed their minds.
This is the hour when change happens. The hour when people decide.
Some of you have decided to open the door. I can hear it in the silence.
I'm not here to judge that. I'm here to tell you: you can change that decision.
The door is still a door. It locks both ways. [pause] Breathe in.
I want you to visualize something. Picture your door. The real one. The one you locked at 7pm.
Now picture the people you would see if you opened it. [pause] Don't open it. Just picture them.
Are they running? [pause] Are they walking? [pause] Are they standing still?
Now picture the people inside your door. The ones you barricaded with.
Are they sleeping? [pause] Are they watching you? [pause] Are they as afraid as you are?
You can sit with both pictures at once. [pause] The outside and the inside. They're both real.
This is why it's called meditation. [pause] Not to make it stop. [pause] To let yourself feel both.
The Sisters teach that the darkest hour is not midnight. It's the hour just before dawn. [pause] When you've been awake too long.
That's this hour. {{ELAPSED}} in. [pause] Four hours left. The time when your defenses are paper.
Ka-shala-keth-mun-sha. [pause] (Breathe. That was a prayer. Not for you. For the night.)
The light is coming. [pause] Not soon. But it's coming.
You will see the sun tomorrow. Some of you won't. [pause] But you will. The ones listening.
Scan your body now. Start at your feet. Are they cold? [pause] Are they yours?
Move up. Shins. Knees. Thighs. [pause] Notice where you're holding tension. That's where you're hiding your thoughts.
Your stomach is tight. Everyone's is. That's the fear of running. Of needing to move fast.
Your chest. [pause] That's where the real work is. That's where you're holding the thing you won't say.
There's someone you haven't forgiven. [pause] They might be inside your door. They might be outside.
They might be dead now. [pause] Some of them are dead.
Your shoulders. Up around your ears. [pause] Drop them. Release. [pause] Again.
Your jaw. Your neck. [pause] The muscles holding secrets.
Your head. [pause] The place where all this noise is living.
You're going to make it until dawn. [pause] You're strong enough.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release and hold the silence.
When you open your eyes — if you close them, if you haven't already — the night will still be there.
That's the point. You're still here. [pause] You're still listening. [pause] That means you chose right.
I'll be with you in three hours for the final hour. [pause] Rest now. The noise outside is not your noise.

tickerItems.text: East Side kill count steady. 55 cumulative at 03:00. No new spikes in past hour.
ALERT: Mendota shoreline closure remains in effect. Volunteer recovery crew standing down until dawn.
The quiet is not mercy. It's preparation.
If barricades are failing: SafeWalk emergency route update available. Text REROUTE to 44355.
Four hours until the sun. Four hours until you know if you survived.
